title: Name age caste vocabulary and rebirth casting are unresolved
claim_or_observation: Current research does not establish one final name etymology numerical age modern caste equivalent or same-actor requirement for Aputhiran and Punniyarajan
notes: Binding casting dialogue translation and publicity safeguard

title: Sali abandons the newborn in a grove
claim_or_observation: Sali travels south gives birth at night and leaves the infant in a grove under conditions of fear and social danger
notes: Verify exact agency danger vocabulary and location

title: A cow feeds and protects the infant
claim_or_observation: A cow hears the baby's cry licks him gives milk and protects him for seven days

title: A learned household adopts and educates him
claim_or_observation: A Brahmin scholar and his wife take the child as their son perform family rites and teach him extensive learning which he remembers
notes: Preserve care and later rejection together

title: Aputhiran protects a cow marked for sacrifice
claim_or_observation: He sees a trembling decorated cow intended for sacrifice intervenes and the frightened animal later gores a man and escapes
notes: Requires exact ritual sequence and animal-welfare review

title: He argues from the cow's lifelong nourishment
claim_or_observation: Aputhiran says the cow ate grass from common land and gave milk to people and asks why it should be harmed
notes: Verify land gift milk and ethical vocabulary

title: He challenges birth-based exclusion through learned examples
claim_or_observation: Aputhiran invokes revered figures associated with animal or complex births and rejects the claim that ancestry determines moral or learned worth
notes: Requires original Tamil caste and polemical review

title: Adoptive belonging collapses under public stigma
claim_or_observation: After Sali's story is disclosed his adoptive father expels him villagers call him a cow thief and stones are thrown into his begging pot
notes: Track public humiliation and prior care

title: Ordinary feeding precedes the miracle
claim_or_observation: In Madurai Aputhiran begs from houses calls blind deaf disabled orphaned and sick people to eat gives them food eats the remainder and sleeps with the pot as pillow

title: Sindhadevi entrusts the inexhaustible vessel
claim_or_observation: During famine Sindhadevi gives Aputhiran a vessel that remains full and he uses it to feed people animals and birds
notes: Verify temple identity vessel language and serving mechanics

title: Aputhiran refuses Indra's reward
claim_or_observation: When Indra offers a boon Aputhiran laughs and argues that the joy of removing hunger exceeds heavenly reward

title: Abundance removes visible hunger and his social role
claim_or_observation: Indra sends rain and prosperity so recipients stop coming and Aputhiran travels through villages asking whether anyone is hungry while people mock him

title: Service identity becomes dependent on visible need
claim_or_observation: Aputhiran's ordinary and miraculous feeding become so central to belonging that the disappearance of recipients destabilises his sense of personal worth

title: He sails toward famine in Javaka
claim_or_observation: Ship travellers report people and creatures dying in a Javaka famine and Aputhiran immediately boards with the vessel to help
notes: Verify geography crew route and urgency

title: The ship leaves him on Manipallavam
claim_or_observation: A storm causes a stop at Manipallavam and the ship departs at night because travellers mistakenly believe Aputhiran has returned aboard
notes: Build exact knowledge and landing continuity

title: He entrusts the vessel to future compassionate hands
claim_or_observation: Finding no recipients Aputhiran places the Amudhasurabhi in Gomukhi pond and directs it to appear for a compassionate protector of life
notes: Central relinquishment rather than ownership evidence

title: Aputhiran fasts and relinquishes his life
claim_or_observation: After placing the vessel in the pond Aputhiran undertakes a fast and dies on Manipallavam
notes: Requires exact death vocabulary and safeguarding review

title: Sacred self-relinquishment is not a universal ethical prescription
claim_or_observation: The adaptation must preserve the poem's karmic meaning while refusing to present self-starvation as the required proof of compassion or a practical solution to purposelessness
notes: Binding direction music edit publicity and safeguarding rule
